seoTitle: SineMicro Products | Automotive MCU Power Management Distributor
seoDescription: Complete SineMicro product portfolio including automotive MCUs, power management ICs, MOSFETs, and IGBTs. Technical specifications and selection guide from authorized distributor.

faqs.question: What SineMicro product categories are available and how do I choose?
faqs.answer: SineMicro offers four main product categories: 1) Automotive MCUs - AEC-Q100 certified microcontrollers for body control, lighting, and motor drive applications. Ideal for turn signals, window controls, and seat controllers. 2) Power Management ICs - High-voltage LDOs and PMICs for automotive power supply applications. Suitable for powering sensors, MCUs, and communication modules. 3) Power Devices - Automotive-grade MOSFETs and IGBTs for power switching and motor control. Used in LED drivers, motor drives, and power distribution. 4) Integrated Solutions - MCU+Power combinations for complete system solutions. Selection depends on your specific application requirements, voltage/current needs, and system architecture. Contact our FAE team for detailed product selection guidance.
faqs.decisionGuide: Identify your application requirements first, then select the appropriate product category. Contact FAE for detailed selection support.

faqs.question: What is the difference between ASM87 and ASM30 MCU series?
faqs.answer: SineMicro ASM87 and ASM30 series serve different application needs: ASM87 Series - General-purpose automotive MCUs optimized for body control applications. Features include: 8-bit or 32-bit cores, integrated ADCs, PWM outputs, CAN/LIN interfaces, wide operating voltage range (2.7V-5.5V), and extended temperature range (-40°C to +125°C). Best for: turn signal controllers, window lift modules, door control modules, and seat controllers. ASM30 Series - Motor control optimized MCUs with enhanced features for motor drive applications. Features include: dedicated motor control peripherals, high-resolution PWM, integrated gate drivers, current sensing amplifiers, and advanced protection features. Best for: BLDC motor control, pump drives, fan controllers, and actuator control. Both series are AEC-Q100 qualified and share development tools for easy migration.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use ASM87 for general body control. Use ASM30 for motor control applications requiring advanced drive capabilities.

faqs.question: How do I select the right power management IC for my automotive application?
faqs.answer: Selecting SineMicro power management ICs involves several key factors: 1) Input Voltage Range - determine the system voltage (typically 12V or 24V automotive battery) and select LDOs with appropriate input voltage ratings (typically 40V-60V max for automotive). 2) Output Voltage - select fixed or adjustable output voltage based on load requirements (common: 3.3V, 5V). 3) Output Current - calculate maximum load current including margins (typically 100mA-500mA for MCU power). 4) Quiescent Current - important for standby power consumption, look for low IQ options (<100uA) for always-on applications. 5) Protection Features - ensure over-current, over-temperature, and reverse polarity protection for automotive reliability. 6) Package - select appropriate package for thermal dissipation and PCB space (SOT-223, TO-252, etc.). SineMicro offers automotive-grade LDOs with various current ratings and features to match your specific requirements.
faqs.decisionGuide: Define voltage, current, and protection requirements first. SineMicro LDOs offer automotive-grade reliability with competitive performance.

faqs.question: What are the key parameters for selecting SineMicro MOSFETs?
faqs.answer: Key parameters for SineMicro MOSFET selection: 1) Voltage Rating (VDS) - must exceed maximum system voltage with margin (typically 40V-100V for 12V automotive systems). 2) Current Rating (ID) - must handle maximum load current with thermal considerations (check SOA curves). 3) On-Resistance (RDS(on)) - lower is better for reduced conduction losses and heating. 4) Gate Threshold (VGS(th)) - ensure compatibility with drive voltage (typically 2.5V-4.5V for logic-level drive). 5) Switching Characteristics - consider switching frequency and requirements for Qg, Ciss for high-frequency applications. 6) Package - select based on thermal requirements and PCB space (SOP-8, DPAK, D2PAK, etc.). 7) Avalanche Rating - important for inductive load switching. SineMicro automotive MOSFETs offer low RDS(on), robust avalanche capability, and AEC-Q101 qualification for reliable automotive operation.
faqs.decisionGuide: Match voltage and current ratings to application requirements. Consider thermal design for high-current applications.

faqs.question: How does SineMicro ensure automotive quality and reliability?
faqs.answer: SineMicro ensures automotive quality through comprehensive processes: 1) AEC-Q100/Q101 Qualification - all automotive products undergo rigorous qualification testing including HTOL (High Temperature Operating Life), temperature cycling, ESD testing, and latch-up testing. 2) Design for Automotive - products designed from ground up for automotive requirements including wide temperature ranges, high EMC immunity, and robust protection features. 3) Manufacturing Control - products manufactured in automotive-qualified facilities with full traceability and statistical process control. 4) Quality Management - IATF 16949 and ISO 9001 certified quality management systems. 5) Field Monitoring - continuous monitoring of field performance and rapid response to any quality issues. 6) PPAP Support - complete Production Part Approval Process documentation available for customer qualification. SineMicro products have demonstrated reliable performance in millions of vehicles from major automotive OEMs.
faqs.decisionGuide: SineMicro products meet automotive quality standards. Contact us for qualification documentation and PPAP support.

faqs.question: What evaluation and development resources are available?
faqs.answer: SineMicro provides comprehensive development resources: 1) Evaluation Kits - complete evaluation boards for MCU and power product families including hardware and sample software. 2) Development Boards - feature-rich development boards with debugging interfaces, expansion connectors, and example circuits. 3) Software Tools - SineMicro Studio IDE, GCC compiler, debugging tools, and flash programming software. 4) SDK and Libraries - peripheral drivers, middleware stacks, and application examples. 5) Reference Designs - complete reference designs for common automotive applications including schematics, BOMs, and documentation. 6) Application Notes - detailed technical documentation for specific applications and design considerations. 7) Technical Training - webinars, workshops, and on-site training available. LiTong Electronics can provide evaluation kits and development resources to qualified customers. Contact our FAE team to request evaluation materials.
faqs.decisionGuide: Complete development ecosystem available. Contact LiTong for evaluation kit requests and technical training.

categories.name: Automotive MCUs
categories.description: SineMicro automotive MCUs are AEC-Q100 certified microcontrollers designed specifically for automotive body electronics applications.
categories.longDescription: SineMicro offers a comprehensive portfolio of automotive-grade microcontrollers designed for body control applications in modern vehicles. As an authorized distributor, LiTong Electronics provides complete selection guidance for SineMicro MCU families. All SineMicro automotive MCUs are AEC-Q100 Grade 1 qualified, ensuring reliable operation from -40°C to +125°C ambient temperature. The MCUs feature automotive-specific peripherals including multiple PWM channels for LED and motor control, high-resolution ADCs for sensor interfacing, and robust communication interfaces including CAN and LIN. With competitive pricing and local technical support, SineMicro MCUs provide an excellent alternative to international suppliers for automotive body electronics applications.

series.name: ASM87 Series
series.description: General-purpose automotive MCUs for body control applications

series.name: ASM30 Series
series.description: Motor control optimized MCUs with advanced drive peripherals

selectionGuide.title: SineMicro Automotive MCU Selection Guide
selectionGuide.description: How to choose the right SineMicro MCU for your automotive application

selectionGuideLink.text: How to Select the Right SineMicro MCU for Your Application
selectionGuideLink.description: Technical guide for selecting optimal SineMicro MCU configuration

faqs.question: What peripherals are included in SineMicro automotive MCUs?
faqs.answer: SineMicro automotive MCUs include comprehensive peripheral sets: 1) Timers and PWM - multiple general-purpose timers and PWM channels for LED dimming and motor control. 2) ADC - 12-bit successive approximation ADC with multiple channels for sensor interfacing. 3) Communication Interfaces - CAN 2.0B controller, LIN master/slave interfaces, UART/SPI/I2C. 4) GPIO - flexible general-purpose I/O with configurable options. 5) Watchdog and Safety - independent watchdog timer, window watchdog, and clock monitoring. 6) Memory - on-chip Flash for program storage and SRAM for data.
faqs.decisionGuide: Evaluate peripheral requirements against your application needs. Contact FAE for specific MCU recommendations.

faqs.question: How do I migrate from other automotive MCUs to SineMicro?
faqs.answer: Migrating to SineMicro MCUs involves: 1) Requirements Analysis - document current MCU features used. 2) Pin Mapping - create mapping between existing MCU pins and SineMicro equivalent pins. 3) Peripheral Configuration - review peripheral differences and adjust initialization code. 4) Code Porting - port application code to SineMicro development environment. 5) Testing - thoroughly test migrated code including functional and environmental testing. 6) Production Programming - set up production programming processes. SineMicro provides migration guides and reference code.
faqs.decisionGuide: Plan migration carefully with thorough testing. SineMicro provides migration support and reference code.

products.name: Automotive 8-bit MCU with 8KB Flash
products.shortDescription: AEC-Q100 certified 8-bit automotive MCU with 8KB Flash, 512B RAM for body control applications.
descriptionParagraphs: The ASM87F0812 is a highly integrated 8-bit automotive microcontroller designed for cost-sensitive body control applications. The device features an enhanced 8051 core running up to 24MHz.
With 8KB of Flash program memory and 512B of SRAM, the ASM87F0812 provides sufficient resources for typical body control applications such as window lift modules and door control modules.

The comprehensive peripheral set includes a 12-bit ADC with 8 channels, 4 PWM channels, UART, SPI, and I2C interfaces. The device operates from 2.7V to 5.5V and is qualified to AEC-Q100 Grade 1 standards.

faeReview.author: Michael Zhang
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - Automotive Electronics
faeReview.content: The ASM87F0812 is an excellent choice for entry-level automotive body control applications. This MCU hits the sweet spot of performance, features, and cost. The enhanced 8051 core provides sufficient processing power for most body control tasks while maintaining code efficiency. For customers looking to reduce BOM costs without compromising quality, the ASM87F0812 is definitely worth considering.
faeReview.insightLogic: The ASM87F0812's key advantage is the optimal balance of features and cost. For typical body control applications, the 8KB Flash and 512B RAM are sufficient. Compared to competitors' offerings, the ASM87F0812 offers similar functionality at significantly lower cost.
faeReview.highlight: Cost-effective 8-bit automotive MCU with optimal feature set for body control

alternativeParts.reason: Higher memory and I/O capacity for more complex applications
alternativeParts.useCase: Use for applications requiring more memory or I/O pins =>< Use ASM87F0812 for simpler applications

companionParts.description: 5V automotive LDO for MCU power supply

faqs.question: What is the typical development timeline for ASM87F0812 projects?
faqs.answer: Typical development timeline: 1) Hardware design - 2-4 weeks for schematic and PCB design. 2) Software development - 4-8 weeks for application code development. 3) Integration and testing - 2-4 weeks for hardware/software integration. 4) EMC and environmental testing - 2-4 weeks for automotive qualification testing. 5) Production preparation - 1-2 weeks for production programming setup. Total timeline from start to production is typically 3-6 months.
faqs.decisionGuide: Typical development timeline 3-6 months. Use reference designs and evaluation boards to accelerate development.

products.name: Automotive 8-bit MCU with 16KB Flash
products.shortDescription: Enhanced 8-bit automotive MCU with 16KB Flash, 1KB RAM for complex body control applications.
descriptionParagraphs: The ASM87F1612 is an enhanced 8-bit automotive microcontroller offering increased memory and peripheral capabilities for more complex body control applications.
Featuring 16KB of Flash program memory and 1KB of SRAM, the ASM87F1612 supports more complex application code and larger data buffers.
Extended peripherals include 6 PWM channels for multi-channel LED or motor control, additional UART and SPI interfaces, and up to 28 GPIO pins.

faeReview.author: David Liu
faeReview.title: Principal FAE - MCU Applications
faeReview.content: The ASM87F1612 is my go-to recommendation for customers needing more resources than the entry-level ASM87F0812 provides. The doubled Flash and RAM capacity makes a significant difference for applications requiring complex control logic. I particularly like is the pin compatibility with the ASM87F0812 - customers can start with the smaller device and upgrade without PCB changes.
faeReview.insightLogic: The ASM87F1612 hits a sweet spot in the product line - enough resources for most body control applications without the cost of 32-bit alternatives. The pin compatibility with ASM87F0812 provides an easy upgrade path.
faeReview.highlight: Enhanced 8-bit MCU with expanded memory and peripherals for complex body control

alternativeParts.reason: Cost-optimized version for applications with lower resource requirements
alternativeParts.useCase: Use for simpler applications with lower memory and I/O requirements =>< Use ASM87F1612 for complex applications

companionParts.description: 5V automotive LDO for MCU power supply

faqs.question: What are the advantages of ASM87F1612 over ASM87F0812?
faqs.answer: Key advantages: 1) Memory - 16KB Flash vs 8KB and 1KB RAM vs 512B. 2) I/O - Up to 28 GPIO pins vs 20 pins. 3) PWM - 6 channels vs 4 channels. 4) Communication - 2 UARTs vs 1, 2 SPIs vs 1. The ASM87F1612 is ideal when your application outgrows the ASM87F0812 resources.
faqs.decisionGuide: Choose ASM87F1612 when you need more memory, I/O, or peripherals. Pin-compatible with ASM87F0812 for flexible designs.

products.name: ARM Cortex-M0 Motor Control MCU
products.shortDescription: 48MHz ARM Cortex-M0 MCU with advanced motor control peripherals for automotive motor applications.
descriptionParagraphs: The ASM30F030 is a dedicated motor control MCU based on ARM Cortex-M0 architecture. With specialized motor control peripherals including PWM timers, ADC synchronization, and encoder interfaces, it simplifies DC and BLDC motor control design.
The 48MHz core provides sufficient processing power for motor control algorithms while maintaining low power consumption. Integrated gate drivers reduce external component count.
AEC-Q100 Grade 1 qualified with -40°C to +125°C operation. Available in LQFP-48 and QFN-48 packages.

faeReview.author: David Chen
faeReview.title: FAE - Motor Control
faeReview.content: The ASM30F030 is purpose-built for motor control applications. The integrated motor control peripherals save significant development time compared to general-purpose MCUs. The PWM synchronization with ADC is particularly useful for current sensing in motor applications. I've used this MCU in window lift and seat control projects with excellent results. The motor control library from SineMicro provides a solid starting point for development.
faeReview.insightLogic: For motor control applications, the ASM30F030 provides the specialized peripherals and processing power needed for smooth, efficient motor control.
faeReview.highlight: Dedicated motor control MCU with specialized peripherals

alternativeParts.reason: Lower cost for simple motor applications
alternativeParts.useCase: Use for simple DC motor control =>< Use ASM30F030 for advanced motor control

faqs.question: What motor types can ASM30F030 control?
faqs.answer: The ASM30F030 supports: 1) Brushed DC motors - single or bidirectional with PWM speed control, 2) BLDC motors - sensored or sensorless with 3-phase PWM, 3) Stepper motors - full and half-step control. The motor control library includes examples for all these motor types.
faqs.decisionGuide: Supports most common automotive motor types. Contact FAE for specific motor control requirements.

products.name: ARM Cortex-M0+ Entry-Level MCU
products.shortDescription: 48MHz ARM Cortex-M0+ MCU with optimized cost-performance for automotive body electronics.
descriptionParagraphs: The ASM32F030 is an entry-level ARM Cortex-M0+ MCU optimized for cost-sensitive automotive applications. It provides modern 32-bit processing at a price point competitive with 8-bit MCUs.

The 48MHz core with single-cycle GPIO enables fast response times for real-time control. The peripheral set includes essential communication interfaces and timers for typical body electronics applications.
AEC-Q100 Grade 1 qualified with -40°C to +125°C operation. Available in TSSOP-20 and QFN-32 packages for space-constrained designs.

faeReview.author: Lisa Wang
faeReview.title: FAE - System Solutions
faeReview.content: The ASM32F030 is perfect for cost-sensitive applications that need 32-bit performance. The price is competitive with 8-bit MCUs while providing ARM ecosystem benefits. I recommend this for simple body electronics like switch monitoring and LED control. The TSSOP-20 package is great for space-constrained modules. The 2KB RAM is sufficient for simple applications but plan carefully if you need larger buffers.
faeReview.insightLogic: For cost-sensitive body electronics, the ASM32F030 provides 32-bit performance at 8-bit prices.
faeReview.highlight: Cost-optimized ARM MCU for entry-level automotive applications

alternativeParts.useCase: Use for legacy design maintenance =>< Use ASM32F030 for new designs

faqs.question: Is 2KB RAM sufficient for my application?
faqs.answer: 2KB RAM is sufficient for: 1) Simple control loops, 2) Small data buffers, 3) Limited variable storage. It may be limiting for: 1) Large communication buffers, 2) Complex data processing, 3) Multiple protocol stacks. Calculate your RAM needs: stack size + global variables + heap requirements. Consider ASM87F1612 (1KB RAM) or ASM32F103 (8KB RAM) if more memory needed.
faqs.decisionGuide: Calculate RAM requirements carefully. 2KB is sufficient for simple applications.

categories.name: Power Management ICs
categories.description: SineMicro power management ICs include high-voltage LDOs and PMICs designed for automotive applications with wide input voltage range and robust protection features.
categories.longDescription: SineMicro power management ICs are specifically designed for the demanding requirements of automotive electronics. As an authorized distributor, LiTong Electronics provides comprehensive selection guidance for SineMicro LDOs and power management solutions. These devices feature wide input voltage ranges to handle automotive battery voltage variations including load dump conditions, low quiescent current for always-on applications, and comprehensive protection features including over-current, over-temperature, and reverse polarity protection. All power management ICs are qualified to automotive standards with AEC-Q100 certification and operate over the full automotive temperature range of -40°C to +125°C.

series.name: SL Series LDOs
series.description: High-voltage automotive LDOs with wide input range and low quiescent current

selectionGuide.title: SineMicro Power Management Selection Guide
selectionGuide.description: How to select the right power management IC for your automotive application

selectionGuideLink.text: How to Select the Right Power Management IC
selectionGuideLink.description: Technical guide for selecting optimal LDO and power management solutions

faqs.question: What protection features are included in SineMicro LDOs?
faqs.answer: SineMicro automotive LDOs include comprehensive protection features: 1) Over-current protection - current limiting protects device and load from excessive current. 2) Thermal shutdown - automatic shutdown if junction temperature exceeds safe limits, with automatic restart when cooled. 3) Reverse polarity protection - protects against reverse battery connection. 4) Load dump protection - withstands automotive load dump transients up to specified voltage. 5) Short-circuit protection - current limiting during output short circuit conditions. 6) Safe operating area protection - ensures device operates within safe limits under all conditions.
faqs.decisionGuide: Comprehensive protection features ensure reliable automotive operation. Evaluate protection requirements for your specific application.

faqs.question: How do I select the right output current rating?
faqs.answer: Selecting LDO output current rating: 1) Calculate maximum load current - sum all load currents including MCU, sensors, and peripherals. 2) Add margin - add 20-30% margin for reliability and transient conditions. 3) Check thermal constraints - ensure package can dissipate heat at maximum current and ambient temperature. 4) Consider peak currents - some loads have high startup currents that must be accommodated. 5) Future expansion - consider future feature additions that may increase current requirements. Example: MCU (50mA) + Sensors (30mA) + Communication (40mA) = 120mA. With 25% margin: 150mA. Select 200mA or 300mA LDO for comfortable margin.
faqs.decisionGuide: Calculate load current with margin for reliable operation. Consider thermal constraints for high-current applications.

products.name: 300mA Automotive LDO with 5V Output
products.shortDescription: AEC-Q100 qualified 300mA LDO with 5V fixed output, wide input range up to 45V for automotive applications.
descriptionParagraphs: The SL7205 is a high-performance automotive LDO designed for powering 5V systems from automotive battery voltage. The device can handle input voltages up to 45V, accommodating load dump conditions in 12V automotive systems.

With a maximum output current of 300mA and low quiescent current of 80uA typical, the SL7205 is suitable for both active operation and always-on applications. The low dropout voltage of 350mV at 300mA ensures efficient operation even at low battery voltages.

Comprehensive protection features include over-current protection, thermal shutdown, reverse polarity protection, and safe operating area protection. The device is available in thermally enhanced SOT-223 and TO-252 packages.

faeReview.author: Lisa Wang
faeReview.title: FAE - Power Management
faeReview.content: The SL7205 is my most recommended automotive LDO for 5V MCU power applications. The 45V maximum input voltage provides excellent margin for automotive load dump conditions, and the 300mA current rating covers most MCU applications with comfortable margin. The low quiescent current of 80uA is excellent for always-on applications where battery drain is a concern. I've used this LDO in numerous automotive designs and it consistently delivers reliable performance. The protection features give peace of mind for automotive applications where robustness is critical.
faeReview.insightLogic: The SL7205 hits the sweet spot for automotive 5V LDOs - enough current for typical MCUs, wide input voltage range for automotive conditions, and low quiescent current for efficiency. The protection features are comprehensive without adding unnecessary cost.
faeReview.highlight: Automotive-grade 5V LDO with wide input range and excellent protection features

alternativeParts.reason: Higher current capability for power-hungry applications
alternativeParts.useCase: Use for applications requiring >300mA current =>< Use SL7205 for standard MCU applications
Use for 3.3V MCU and sensor applications =>< Use SL7205 for 5V systems

companionParts.description: 5V automotive MCU powered by this LDO

faqs.question: What is the dropout voltage and why does it matter?
faqs.answer: Dropout voltage is the minimum input-to-output voltage differential required for the LDO to maintain regulation. For SL7205: 350mV at 300mA load. This matters because: 1) Minimum Input Voltage - output voltage + dropout = minimum input voltage for regulation. For 5V output: 5V + 0.35V = 5.35V minimum input. 2) Battery Conditions - during cold cranking, battery voltage can drop below 6V. Low dropout ensures continued operation. 3) Efficiency - lower dropout means less power dissipation in the LDO. 4) Battery Life - less voltage headroom required extends battery life. The SL7205's 350mV dropout is excellent for automotive applications where battery voltage variations are significant.
faqs.decisionGuide: Low dropout voltage important for automotive applications with battery voltage variations. SL7205 offers excellent dropout performance.

faqs.question: How do I calculate power dissipation and thermal design?
faqs.answer: Power dissipation calculation for LDO: Pd = (Vin - Vout) × Iout + (Vin × Iq). Example: Vin = 14V, Vout = 5V, Iout = 200mA, Iq = 80uA. Pd = (14-5) × 0.2 + (14 × 0.00008) = 1.8 + 0.001 = 1.8W. Thermal design: Junction temperature TJ = TA + (Pd × θJA). For SOT-223 with θJA = 60°C/W at TA = 85°C: TJ = 85 + (1.8 × 60) = 193°C (exceeds limit). Solutions: 1) Use TO-252 package with better thermal performance, 2) Reduce input voltage if possible, 3) Reduce load current, 4) Add external heatsinking, 5) Use switching regulator for high differential applications. For high Vin-Vout differentials with high current, consider switching regulators instead of LDOs.
faqs.decisionGuide: Calculate power dissipation and junction temperature. Use appropriate package or consider switching regulator for high differential applications.

products.name: 300mA Automotive LDO with 3.3V Output
products.shortDescription: AEC-Q100 qualified 300mA LDO with 3.3V fixed output for modern low-voltage automotive systems.
descriptionParagraphs: The SL7333 provides regulated 3.3V output for modern automotive electronics that use 3.3V logic levels. The device handles input voltages up to 45V, making it suitable for 12V and 24V automotive systems.

With 300mA output capability and low 80uA quiescent current, the SL7333 efficiently powers 3.3V MCUs, sensors, and communication modules. The 280mV dropout voltage at 300mA ensures regulation even during low battery conditions.
The device includes the same comprehensive protection features as the SL7205, ensuring reliable operation in harsh automotive environments. Available in SOT-223 and TO-252 packages.

faeReview.author: Lisa Wang
faeReview.title: FAE - Power Management
faeReview.content: The SL7333 is essential for modern automotive designs using 3.3V logic. Many newer automotive MCUs and communication ICs operate at 3.3V, and this LDO provides clean, reliable power. The lower dropout voltage compared to 5V versions is beneficial for battery-powered applications. I've successfully used this LDO in designs with 3.3V ARM-based automotive MCUs and CAN transceivers. The protection features and wide temperature range make it suitable for under-hood applications.
faeReview.insightLogic: The SL7333 fills the need for 3.3V automotive LDOs as the industry moves to lower voltage logic. The specifications mirror the popular SL7205, making it easy for customers to migrate between voltage options.
faeReview.highlight: 3.3V automotive LDO for modern low-voltage automotive electronics

comparison.Dropout: Higher dropout due to higher voltage

alternativeParts.useCase: Use for 5V MCU and sensor applications =>< Use SL7333 for 3.3V systems

companionParts.description: 3.3V ARM MCU powered by this LDO

faqs.question: When should I use 3.3V vs 5V for automotive applications?
faqs.answer: 3.3V vs 5V selection considerations: Use 3.3V when: 1) Using modern MCUs and ICs that operate at 3.3V, 2) Power consumption is critical (lower voltage = lower power), 3) Interfacing with 3.3V sensors and modules, 4) New designs where 5V legacy compatibility not required. Use 5V when: 1) Legacy system compatibility required, 2) Using 5V sensors or actuators, 3) Higher noise immunity needed (5V has better SNR), 4) Long cable runs where voltage drop is a concern. Many modern automotive designs use mixed voltages - 5V for power devices and sensors, 3.3V for digital logic and communication. Level shifters may be needed for interfacing between voltage domains.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use 3.3V for modern designs with 3.3V components. Use 5V for legacy compatibility or when higher voltage beneficial.

products.name: 500mA Automotive LDO with 5V Output
products.shortDescription: AEC-Q100 qualified 500mA high-current LDO with 5V fixed output for power-hungry automotive applications.
descriptionParagraphs: The SL7210 is a high-current automotive LDO designed for applications requiring more power than the standard 300mA devices. With 500mA output capability, it can power multiple MCUs, sensors, and communication modules from a single regulator.

The device maintains the same 45V maximum input voltage and comprehensive protection features as the SL7205, while delivering higher current. The low dropout voltage of 300mV at 500mA ensures efficient operation.
Available in thermally enhanced TO-252 and TO-263 packages to handle the increased power dissipation. AEC-Q100 Grade 1 qualified for automotive reliability.

faeReview.author: Lisa Wang
faeReview.title: FAE - Power Management
faeReview.content: The SL7210 is my recommendation when customers need more than 300mA from their automotive LDO. The 500mA capability handles multiple loads without needing separate regulators. The larger packages (TO-252/TO-263) provide better heat sinking for the higher power dissipation. I have used this part in gateway modules and body control modules where multiple MCUs and communication transceivers need power. The protection features and wide temperature range make it reliable for automotive use.
faeReview.insightLogic: For high-current automotive applications, the SL7210 provides the extra current capability while maintaining automotive-grade reliability.
faeReview.highlight: High-current 500mA automotive LDO for demanding applications

alternativeParts.reason: Lower current for smaller, cost-sensitive designs
alternativeParts.useCase: Use for <300mA applications =>< Use SL7210 for high-current needs

faqs.question: How much PCB copper area is needed for SL7210?
faqs.answer: For SL7210 at maximum current: TO-252 package needs minimum 1 square inch copper area for heat sinking. TO-263 package with tab can handle higher dissipation with 0.5 square inches. Calculate thermal requirements: P = (Vin - Vout) × Iout. At 14V input, 5V output, 500mA: P = 4.5W. Ensure maximum junction temperature stays below 125°C at your ambient temperature.
faqs.decisionGuide: Provide adequate copper area based on power dissipation calculations. Use thermal vias for better heat spreading.

products.name: High-Voltage 3.3V LDO with 60V Input
products.shortDescription: AEC-Q100 qualified 300mA LDO with extended 60V input range for 24V truck and heavy vehicle applications.
descriptionParagraphs: The SL7333H is a high-voltage LDO specifically designed for 24V automotive and heavy vehicle applications. The extended 60V input range accommodates severe load dump conditions in 24V systems.

With 300mA output capability and 3.3V fixed output, it efficiently powers modern low-voltage electronics in trucks, buses, and construction equipment. The 100uA quiescent current minimizes battery drain.
Comprehensive protection features include over-current, thermal shutdown, and reverse polarity protection. Available in TO-252 and TO-263 packages for thermal management.

faeReview.author: David Chen
faeReview.title: FAE - Heavy Vehicle Applications
faeReview.content: The SL7333H is essential for 24V vehicle applications. Standard 45V LDOs are not sufficient for 24V systems where load dumps can exceed 60V. This LDO provides the voltage margin needed for reliable operation. I've used it in truck body electronics and construction equipment where 24V is standard. The 3.3V output is compatible with modern MCUs and sensors. The protection features are critical in the harsh electrical environment of heavy vehicles.
faeReview.insightLogic: For 24V automotive and heavy vehicle applications, the SL7333H provides the necessary voltage rating and automotive reliability.
faeReview.highlight: 60V input LDO for 24V truck and heavy vehicle applications

alternativeParts.useCase: Use for 12V automotive =>< Use SL7333H for 24V/heavy vehicle

faqs.question: Why do 24V systems need 60V LDOs?
faqs.answer: 24V vehicle systems experience load dump transients that can reach 60V or higher. Load dump occurs when a charged inductor (alternator field winding) loses its load, causing a voltage spike. Standard 45V LDOs would be damaged by these transients. The SL7333H's 60V rating provides adequate margin for reliable operation in 24V systems.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use 60V LDOs for 24V systems. Use 45V LDOs for 12V systems.

categories.name: Power Devices
categories.description: SineMicro power devices include automotive-grade MOSFETs and IGBTs designed for power switching and motor control applications in vehicles.
categories.longDescription: SineMicro power devices deliver reliable power switching solutions for automotive applications. The portfolio includes N-channel and P-channel MOSFETs with low on-resistance and high current capability, suitable for LED drivers, motor control, and power distribution. All devices are AEC-Q101 qualified and designed to withstand the harsh automotive environment including load dump conditions and wide temperature variations.

selectionGuide.description: How to select the right MOSFET for your automotive power application

selectionGuideLink.description: Technical guide for selecting optimal MOSFET and power switch solutions

faqs.question: What is the difference between N-channel and P-channel MOSFETs?
faqs.answer: N-channel MOSFETs require positive gate voltage to turn on and are typically used for low-side switching. They generally have lower RDS(on) and are more cost-effective. P-channel MOSFETs require negative gate voltage relative to source and are used for high-side switching. N-channel is preferred for most applications due to better performance, but P-channel simplifies high-side switch design.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use N-channel for low-side switching and best performance. Use P-channel for simple high-side switching.

products.name: 40V/10A N-Channel Automotive MOSFET
products.shortDescription: AEC-Q101 qualified N-channel MOSFET with 40V VDS, 10A ID, and 15mΩ RDS(on) for automotive switching.
descriptionParagraphs: The SM4010N is an automotive-grade N-channel MOSFET designed for power switching applications in vehicles. With a 40V drain-source voltage rating and 10A continuous current capability, it handles typical automotive loads with ease.
The low on-resistance of 15mΩ minimizes conduction losses and heat generation, improving system efficiency. The logic-level gate threshold allows direct drive from 3.3V or 5V MCUs.
AEC-Q101 qualification ensures reliable operation over the automotive temperature range. The device is available in SOP-8 and DPAK packages for different thermal requirements.

faeReview.author: David Chen
faeReview.title: FAE - Power Applications
faeReview.content: The SM4010N is an excellent general-purpose automotive MOSFET. The 40V rating provides good margin for 12V automotive systems, and the 10A current capability handles most body control loads. The 15mΩ RDS(on) keeps losses low. I particularly like the logic-level gate drive - it works well with modern 3.3V MCUs without needing level shifters. The AEC-Q101 qualification gives confidence for automotive use. I've used this part in LED driver and motor control applications with excellent results.
faeReview.insightLogic: The SM4010N hits a sweet spot for automotive switching - good voltage margin, adequate current, low resistance, and logic-level drive. It's a versatile part that works in many applications.
faeReview.highlight: Versatile 40V/10A automotive MOSFET with low RDS(on) and logic-level drive

alternativeParts.reason: Higher current capability for demanding applications
alternativeParts.useCase: Use for applications requiring >10A current =>< Use SM4010N for standard loads

faqs.question: How do I calculate power dissipation in the MOSFET?
faqs.answer: Power dissipation in MOSFET is calculated as P = I² × RDS(on) for conduction losses. For 10A current and 15mΩ RDS(on): P = 10² × 0.015 = 1.5W. Also consider switching losses at high frequencies. Ensure adequate PCB copper area for heat sinking - typically 1 square inch per watt.
faqs.decisionGuide: Calculate conduction and switching losses. Provide adequate thermal management.

products.name: 40V/15A N-Channel Automotive MOSFET
products.shortDescription: High-current AEC-Q101 MOSFET with 40V VDS, 15A ID, and 10mΩ RDS(on) for demanding automotive applications.
descriptionParagraphs: The SM4015N is a high-current automotive N-channel MOSFET designed for demanding power switching applications. With 15A continuous current capability and low 10mΩ RDS(on), it handles high-power loads efficiently.
The 40V voltage rating provides margin for 12V automotive systems including load dump conditions. The enhanced gate drive characteristics ensure reliable switching even at high currents.
Available in DPAK and D2PAK packages for different thermal requirements. AEC-Q101 qualified for automotive reliability.

faeReview.author: David Chen
faeReview.title: FAE - Power Applications
faeReview.content: The SM4015N is my go-to part for high-current automotive switching. The 15A capability and 10mΩ resistance make it efficient even at high loads. I've used it in seat heater controls, high-power LED drivers, and motor applications. The D2PAK package with good PCB copper handles the heat well. The gate drive is well-characterized and works reliably with automotive MCU outputs.
faeReview.insightLogic: For high-current automotive loads, the SM4015N provides the current capability and low resistance needed for efficient operation. Proper thermal design is important at maximum current.
faeReview.highlight: High-current 15A MOSFET with low 10mΩ RDS(on) for demanding applications

alternativeParts.reason: Lower current and smaller package for cost-sensitive designs
alternativeParts.useCase: Use for <10A applications =>< Use SM4015N for high-current needs

faqs.question: What package should I choose for high current?
faqs.answer: For high current applications: DPAK handles up to ~10-12A with good PCB copper. D2PAK handles 15A+ with adequate thermal design. TO-220 and TO-263 are also options for through-hole or high-power surface mount. Consider thermal resistance from junction to ambient for your specific PCB design.
faqs.decisionGuide: Choose package based on current and thermal requirements. D2PAK recommended for 15A operation.

products.name: 60V/20A P-Channel Automotive MOSFET
products.shortDescription: High-voltage P-channel MOSFET with 60V VDS, 20A ID for high-side switching in automotive applications.
descriptionParagraphs: The SM6020P is a high-voltage P-channel MOSFET designed for high-side switching applications in automotive systems. The 60V rating accommodates 24V truck systems and load dump conditions.
With 20A current capability and 25mΩ RDS(on), it handles high-power high-side switching efficiently. The P-channel configuration simplifies high-side switch design without needing charge pumps.
AEC-Q101 qualified with -40°C to +150°C operating range. Available in TO-252 and TO-263 packages.

faeReview.author: David Chen
faeReview.title: FAE - Power Applications
faeReview.content: The SM6020P is an excellent high-side switch solution. The P-channel eliminates the need for complex gate drive circuits - just pull the gate low to turn on. The 60V rating handles 24V truck applications and load dumps. I've used it in battery disconnect switches and power distribution modules. The 20A capability is generous for most automotive loads. Just remember that P-channel has higher RDS(on) than equivalent N-channel, so account for that in thermal design.
faeReview.insightLogic: P-channel MOSFETs simplify high-side switching design. The SM6020P's 60V rating and high current make it versatile for automotive power distribution.
faeReview.highlight: High-voltage 60V P-channel MOSFET for easy high-side switching

alternativeParts.reason: N-channel with driver for best performance
alternativeParts.useCase: Use when lowest RDS(on) critical =>< Use SM6020P for simple high-side switch

faqs.question: Why use P-channel instead of N-channel for high-side?
faqs.answer: P-channel simplifies high-side switching because it turns on by pulling gate low (to ground), which is easy to do. N-channel on high-side requires gate voltage above source (which is at supply voltage), typically requiring a charge pump or bootstrap circuit. Trade-off is P-channel has higher RDS(on) and cost than equivalent N-channel.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use P-channel for simple designs. Use N-channel with driver when best performance needed.

products.name: 100V/5A N-Channel Automotive MOSFET
products.shortDescription: High-voltage N-channel MOSFET with 100V VDS, 5A ID for load dump protection and high-voltage switching.
descriptionParagraphs: The SM1005N is a high-voltage N-channel MOSFET designed for applications requiring voltage margin above standard 40V devices. The 100V rating handles severe load dump conditions and 48V mild-hybrid systems.
With 5A current capability and 45mΩ RDS(on), it provides reliable switching for protection circuits and power control. The logic-level gate drive works with standard MCU outputs.
AEC-Q101 qualified for automotive use. Available in SOP-8 and DPAK packages.

faeReview.author: David Chen
faeReview.title: FAE - Power Applications
faeReview.content: The SM1005N provides extra voltage margin for demanding automotive conditions. The 100V rating handles load dumps that can exceed 60V in some systems. I use it in protection circuits and for 48V mild-hybrid applications. The 5A current is adequate for many control circuits. The higher RDS(on) compared to 40V parts is the trade-off for voltage margin, but for lower current applications this is acceptable.
faeReview.insightLogic: When voltage margin is critical, the SM1005N's 100V rating provides confidence. Use for protection circuits and 48V systems.
faeReview.highlight: High-voltage 100V MOSFET for load dump protection and 48V systems

alternativeParts.reason: Lower voltage but higher current and better resistance
alternativeParts.useCase: Use for standard 12V when 100V not needed =>< Use SM1005N for high voltage

faqs.question: When do I need 100V MOSFET in automotive?
faqs.answer: 100V MOSFETs are needed for: 1) Load dump protection circuits - load dumps can exceed 60V, 2) 48V mild-hybrid systems - need margin above 48V, 3) Severe electrical environment - provides extra safety margin, 4) Protection circuits - where device must survive worst-case transients. Standard 12V body electronics typically use 40V MOSFETs.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use 100V for protection circuits, 48V systems, or severe environments. Use 40V for standard 12V body electronics.

categories.name: Integrated Solutions
categories.description: SineMicro integrated solutions combine MCUs and power management in optimized configurations for complete system solutions.
categories.longDescription: SineMicro integrated solutions provide complete system-level solutions combining MCUs, power management, and power devices in optimized configurations. These solutions are designed for specific automotive applications such as body control modules, LED lighting systems, and motor control units. Each solution includes reference designs, software libraries, and application notes to accelerate development.

series.name: SIM Series
series.description: System-in-Module solutions with integrated MCU and power

selectionGuide.title: SineMicro Integrated Solution Selection Guide
selectionGuide.description: How to select the right integrated solution for your automotive application

selectionGuideLink.text: How to Select the Right Integrated Solution
selectionGuideLink.description: Technical guide for selecting optimal system solutions

faqs.question: What are the benefits of integrated solutions?
faqs.answer: Integrated solutions provide: 1) Faster time to market - pre-validated hardware and software, 2) Reduced risk - proven design reduces debugging time, 3) Optimized performance - components selected and configured for best performance, 4) Complete support - reference designs and software libraries included, 5) Cost optimization - BOM optimized for the specific application.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use integrated solutions when time to market is critical or when you want a proven starting point for your design.

products.name: Body Control Module Reference Design
products.shortDescription: Complete body control module solution with ASM87 MCU, power management, and MOSFET drivers for door/window control.
descriptionParagraphs: The SIM-BCM-01 is a complete reference design for automotive body control modules. It includes an ASM87F1612 MCU, SL7205 and SL7333 LDOs for power management, and SM4010N MOSFETs for load driving.
The design supports door lock control, window lift, mirror adjustment, and seat control functions. Complete with schematics, PCB layout, BOM, and software libraries.
AEC-Q100 and AEC-Q101 qualified components ensure automotive reliability. Reference design documentation includes application notes and test reports.

faeReview.author: Lisa Wang
faeReview.title: FAE - System Solutions
faeReview.content: The SIM-BCM-01 is an excellent starting point for body control module development. The combination of ASM87 MCU with proven power management and driver circuits saves significant development time. The reference design is well-documented and the software libraries are comprehensive. I've seen customers go from concept to working prototype in weeks rather than months using this solution. The component selection is optimized for automotive body electronics.
faeReview.insightLogic: For body control applications, the SIM-BCM-01 provides a proven foundation that accelerates development and reduces risk.
faeReview.highlight: Complete BCM reference design with MCU, power, and drivers

alternativeParts.useCase: Use when unique requirements not met by reference =>< Use SIM-BCM-01 for standard applications

faqs.question: Can I modify the reference design for my specific needs?
faqs.answer: Yes, reference designs are starting points that can be modified. Common modifications include: changing I/O configuration, adding sensors, modifying power requirements, and adding communication interfaces. The reference design documentation includes guidelines for modifications. LiTong FAE can provide support for customization.
faqs.decisionGuide: Start with reference design and modify as needed. Contact FAE for customization support.

products.name: LED Lighting Control Module
products.shortDescription: Complete LED lighting solution with MCU, constant current drivers, and protection for automotive lighting applications.
descriptionParagraphs: The SIM-LED-01 is a comprehensive LED lighting control solution for automotive applications. It includes an ASM87F0812 MCU for control, SM4010N MOSFETs for LED switching, and integrated current sensing.
The design supports turn signals, brake lights, running lights, and interior lighting. Features include PWM dimming, fault detection, and thermal management.
Complete with schematics, PCB layout optimized for thermal performance, BOM, and software with lighting control algorithms.

faeReview.author: Lisa Wang
faeReview.title: FAE - System Solutions
faeReview.content: The SIM-LED-01 provides everything needed for automotive LED lighting. The PWM dimming works smoothly and the fault detection catches LED failures. The thermal design handles high-power LED strings well. The software includes algorithms for smooth turn signal sequencing and brake light brightness control. It's a complete solution that gets customers to market quickly.
faeReview.insightLogic: For LED lighting applications, the SIM-LED-01 provides proven hardware and software that handles the unique requirements of automotive lighting.
faeReview.highlight: Complete LED lighting solution with dimming and protection

alternativeParts.reason: Custom design for unique LED configurations
alternativeParts.useCase: Use for non-standard LED arrangements =>< Use SIM-LED-01 for standard lighting

faqs.question: How many LEDs can each channel drive?
faqs.answer: Each channel can drive up to 2A of LED current. The number of LEDs depends on LED forward voltage and configuration. For typical 3V LEDs: Series string of 3 LEDs at 12V input. Parallel strings can increase total LED count. The design includes current sensing for each channel to maintain consistent brightness.
faqs.decisionGuide: Calculate LED configuration based on forward voltage and current requirements. Reference design includes examples.

products.name: DC Motor Control Module
products.shortDescription: Complete DC motor control solution with MCU, H-bridge drivers, and protection for automotive motor applications.
descriptionParagraphs: The SIM-MOT-01 is a complete DC motor control solution for automotive applications. It includes an ASM30F030 motor control MCU, SM4015N MOSFETs in H-bridge configuration, and current sensing for stall detection.
The design supports window lift, seat adjustment, mirror positioning, and pump control. Features include PWM speed control, current limiting, stall detection, and over-temperature protection.
Complete with schematics, PCB layout, BOM, and motor control software libraries.

faeReview.author: David Chen
faeReview.title: FAE - Motor Control
faeReview.content: The SIM-MOT-01 provides a solid foundation for DC motor control. The ASM30 MCU has excellent motor control peripherals and the H-bridge using SM4015N MOSFETs handles typical automotive motors well. The stall detection and current limiting protect both motor and driver. The software includes ramp-up/down algorithms for smooth operation. I've used this in window lift and seat control applications with excellent results.
faeReview.insightLogic: For DC motor control, the SIM-MOT-01 provides proven hardware and control algorithms that handle the demanding requirements of automotive motors.
faeReview.highlight: Complete motor control solution with H-bridge and protection

alternativeParts.useCase: Use for simple on/off control =>< Use SIM-MOT-01 for full-featured control

faqs.question: What motor types can this control?
faqs.answer: The SIM-MOT-01 controls brushed DC motors commonly used in automotive applications. It supports: 1) Standard DC motors - single direction with PWM speed control, 2) Reversible DC motors - H-bridge configuration for bidirectional control, 3) Various power levels - up to 15A peak with appropriate heat sinking. Not suitable for brushless DC (BLDC) or stepper motors which require different drive electronics.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use for brushed DC motors in automotive applications. Contact FAE for BLDC motor control solutions.

products.name: Sensor Interface Module
products.shortDescription: Complete sensor interface solution with MCU, signal conditioning, and power for automotive sensor applications.
descriptionParagraphs: The SIM-SNS-01 is a comprehensive sensor interface solution for automotive applications. It includes an ASM87F0812 MCU, precision analog front-end, and power management optimized for sensor applications.
The design supports various sensor types including temperature, pressure, position, and current sensors. Features include programmable gain amplification, filtering, and diagnostic functions.
Complete with schematics, PCB layout optimized for analog performance, BOM, and sensor interface software libraries.

faeReview.author: Dr. Michael Zhang
faeReview.title: FAE - Sensor Systems
faeReview.content: The SIM-SNS-01 provides an excellent platform for automotive sensor interfaces. The analog front-end has good noise performance and the programmable gain allows interfacing with various sensor types. The software includes calibration routines and diagnostic functions. I've used this for temperature monitoring, pressure sensing, and current measurement applications. The reference design handles the analog layout considerations well.
faeReview.insightLogic: For sensor interface applications, the SIM-SNS-01 provides proven analog signal conditioning and software that simplifies sensor integration.
faeReview.highlight: Complete sensor interface with signal conditioning and diagnostics

comparison.Cost: May be lower for high volume

alternativeParts.useCase: Use for high-volume single-sensor applications =>< Use SIM-SNS-01 for multi-sensor flexibility

faqs.question: What sensor types are supported?
faqs.answer: The SIM-SNS-01 supports various sensor types: 1) Analog voltage output - temperature sensors, pressure sensors, position sensors, 2) Current output - 4-20mA industrial sensors, 3) Resistance-based - thermistors, RTDs, 4) Frequency output - variable reluctance sensors, 5) Digital - SPI/I2C smart sensors. The programmable gain and filtering accommodate different signal levels and noise requirements.
faqs.decisionGuide: Supports most common automotive sensor types. Contact FAE for specific sensor interface requirements.
